description: 4 HIGH npm audit warnings (hono, express-rate-limit, path-to-regexp, @hono/node-server)
export_memory output_path accepts arbitrary file paths without validation — can write to any location
import_memory source_path accepts arbitrary file paths without validation — can read any SQLite file
execSync with string interpolation of mcpConfigPath — shell metacharacters in path enable RCE
execSync with string interpolation of mcpConfigPath — same pattern as checkpoint
agent_name passed to resolveHmemPathLegacy constructs paths via path.join without sanitization
Curator role check relies solely on HMEM_AGENT_ROLE env var — any process can set it
delete_agent_memory falls back to HMEM_PATH when agent not found — bypasses curator check for self-deletion
Predictable /tmp paths with session data, no restrictive permissions
catch(e) returns raw error message including file paths, DB details, stack traces
LIMIT ${limit} string interpolation — NOT exploitable (Zod validates as number) but fragile anti-pattern
hono <=4.12.6: prototype pollution, arbitrary file access, SSE injection, cookie injection
path-to-regexp 8.0.0-8.3.0: ReDoS via wildcards, DoS via optional groups
No logging of security events: delete, update, import operations have no audit trail
Sync tokens stored in plaintext JSON — chmod 0o600 with silent failure on error
